steps.description: Face the front end of the Ø65 mm cast iron bar to establish a clean Z-zero reference point and remove excess material from the 150 mm raw length.
Rough and finish turn the outer diameter of the bar from Ø65 mm to Ø60 mm for a total length of 135 mm from the front face.
Perform step turning to reduce the diameter from Ø60 mm to Ø40 mm. This cut starts from the front face and extends 105 mm deep (covering the taper, the two Ø40 sections, and the groove area).
Apply taper turning at the front of the part. The tool will move from Ø20 mm at the face (Z0) to Ø40 mm at a distance of 40 mm (Z-40).
Plunge the grooving tool at a distance of 70 mm from the front face (Z-70) to create a groove with a diameter of Ø30 mm and a width of 10 mm.
Cut the finished part from the remaining raw stock at the 135 mm mark (Z-135) to achieve the final part length.

reasoning: The manufacturing strategy follows a 'large-to-small' diameter approach to maintain maximum workpiece rigidity during machining. After establishing a reference face, the entire part profile is roughed out to the maximum diameter (Ø60). Then, the secondary diameter (Ø40) is established. Specialized features like the taper and the internal groove are machined afterward because they require the base diameters to be set. Finally, the part is separated from the raw stock using a parting tool to ensure the total length matches the 135 mm requirement derived from the sum of the individual section lengths (30+25+10+30+40).
step_by_step_plan: 1. Secure the Ø65 mm cast iron bar in the lathe chuck with approximately 145 mm protruding. 2. Mount the facing tool and face the end to set the Z-axis datum. 3. Use the turning tool to reduce the bar to Ø60 mm for a length of 135 mm. 4. Continue with the turning tool to create a step of Ø40 mm starting from the front face and ending at 105 mm from the face. 5. Adjust the tool path (or compound slide) to cut a taper from Ø20 mm at the tip to Ø40 mm over a 40 mm length. 6. Switch to a 10 mm wide grooving tool; position it 70 mm from the front face and feed in radially until the diameter reaches Ø30 mm. 7. Finally, use the parting tool at 135 mm from the front face to cut through the bar and complete the part.
